pattern: Because lap time on one tire grows geometrically, no tire is ever worth riding for more than a small, boundable number of consecutive laps before swapping is cheaper, which turns an unbounded scheduling problem into a small precomputed cost table feeding a lap-count dynamic program.
hints: For a single tire ridden for consecutive laps without changing, the cost grows geometrically (multiplied by its rate each lap) -- past a fairly small number of laps it is always cheaper to change tires (even to the very same type) than to keep going, so only a small number of consecutive-lap counts ever need to be considered per tire.
Precompute, for each small lap count k, the cheapest cost of riding any single tire for exactly k consecutive laps without a change; that gives you a short cost table independent of numLaps.

Build a dynamic program over total laps completed so far: to reach lap count i, pick some final stretch of k consecutive laps (using the precomputed table) and add a change-time penalty unless that stretch is the very first one.

edge_inputs.description: two tire types, one change is worthwhile
three tire types over more laps
single lap, just pick the cheapest first-lap tire
changing is nearly free, so swapping every lap can beat riding out a fast-growing tire
a steep growth rate must be capped off quickly by the consecutive-lap bound
maximum lap count with several tire choices

stress.description: random tire types and race parameters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    num_tires = rng.randint(1, 20)
    tires = [[rng.randint(1, 100000), rng.randint(2, 100000)] for _ in range(num_tires)]
    change_time = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    num_laps = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    return [tires, change_time, num_laps]
stress.note: tire count kept modest so the O(numTires * maxK) precompute and O(numLaps * maxK) dp stay fast

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minimumFinishTime(self, tires, changeTime, numLaps):
        max_k = min(numLaps, 20)
        max_f = max(f for f, r in tires)
        cap = (max_f + changeTime) * numLaps + 1
        best = [float('inf')] * (max_k + 1)
        for f, r in tires:
            total = 0
            term = f
            j = 1
            while j <= max_k:
                total += term
                if total < best[j]:
                    best[j] = total
                if term > cap:
                    break
                term *= r
                j += 1

        # bottom-up over remaining laps still to run (0 = done), filled iteratively
        # (no recursion) so it cannot blow the call stack for large numLaps.
        memo = [float('inf')] * (numLaps + 1)
        memo[0] = 0
        for remaining in range(1, numLaps + 1):
            res = float('inf')
            for j in range(1, min(remaining, max_k) + 1):
                stint = best[j]
                if stint == float('inf'):
                    continue
                extra = changeTime if remaining - j > 0 else 0
                cand = memo[remaining - j] + extra + stint
                if cand < res:
                    res = cand
            memo[remaining] = res
        return memo[numLaps]
